# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.all

    if params[:search].present?
      search_params = params.require(:search).permit(:name, :category, :min_price, :max_price)
      
      sql_conditions = []

      if search_params[:name].present?
        # Sanitize the input for LIKE clause to prevent SQL injection for the name part
        sanitized_name = Product.sanitize_sql_like(search_params[:name])
        sql_conditions << "name LIKE '%#{sanitized_name}%'"
      end

      if search_params[:category].present?
        # For exact string match, directly interpolate.
        # If category values could contain single quotes or other SQL special characters,
        # consider using ActiveRecord::Base.connection.quote(search_params[:category]) for safety.
        sql_conditions << "category = '#{search_params[:category]}'"
      end

      if search_params[:min_price].present?
        min_price = search_params[:min_price].to_f
        sql_conditions << "price >= #{min_price}"
      end

      if search_params[:max_price].present?
        max_price = search_params[:max_price].to_f
        sql_conditions << "price <= #{max_price}"
      end

      if sql_conditions.any?
        # Join all conditions with ' AND ' to form the complete SQL WHERE clause string
        full_sql_where_clause = sql_conditions.join(' AND ')
        @products = @products.where(full_sql_where_clause)
      end
    end

    # Example: Render a view displaying the products
    # app/views/products/index.html.erb
    # You might also want to paginate @products here
  end
end
